content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

January 26, 2005

Thomas A. Willis
Remcho, Johansen & Purcell
201 Delores Avenue
San Leandro, CA 94577

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-04-224

Dear Mr. Willis:

This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the California Voter Registration 2002 Committee for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).1 Pursuant to our telephone conversation on January 25, 2005, the California Voter Registration 2002 Committee has decided not to engage in independent expenditures in support of, or in opposition to candidates and will remain engaged solely in partisan voter education and registration activities. Therefore, the advice requested from the Fair Political Practices Commission is no longer required as the activity in question will not occur.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Galena West
Counsel, Legal Division

GW:jg

1 Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
